QUESTION
column7: STRATEGIC FUNCTION

column5: Agent Simmons, you authored the Affidavit of Probable Cause in this case?
column7: Establish ownership of the document.

column5: Paragraph [X] states [specific claim]. What is the source of that information?
column7: Force her to identify Groff-sourced paragraphs vs. personal knowledge.

column5: Have you authored affidavits in other cases? How many approximately?
column7: Template detection setup. If many, next question compares language.

column5: The language in paragraph [X] of this AOPC — have you used substantially similar language in other affidavits?
column7: If yes = template. If denies, produce other affidavits showing identical paragraphs.

column5: Did you independently verify each factual assertion before swearing to it?
column7: Lock her in. Every contradiction from here = either she lied or didn't verify.

column5: The AOPC references call volume to establish the scope of the alleged scheme, correct?
column7: Foundation for Five9 data.

column5: How many calls did the government represent were made?
column7: Lock in the number.

column5: Agent Simmons, I have the complete Five9 export — 1,182,156 files. 77.6% were failed connections under 60 seconds. Only 9.2% — roughly 108,000 — were actual calls over 5 minutes. Were you aware of this when you swore the affidavit?
column6: No / I was not aware of the specific breakdown.
column7: The kill shot. She either knew (deliberate misrepresentation) or didn't know (reckless disregard). Both satisfy Franks.

column5: So when the government represented '1.1 million calls' — the actual number of substantive calls was approximately 108,000. A 10.9x inflation. Is that correct?
column7: She can't deny it. The data is machine-generated. Her confirmation or denial is irrelevant. Schmehl has the numbers.

column5: You were present at the United States Courthouse in Reading, Pennsylvania on May 8, 2025, the date of the superseding indictment hearing?
column7: Establish presence.

column5: AUSA Samuel Dalke was also present in the hallway outside the courtroom?
column7: Confirm Dalke's presence.

column5: After the hearing, I approached AUSA Dalke in the hallway. You observed that?
column7: Establish the approach.

column5: What did I do when I reached him?
column6: You shook his hand / You introduced yourself.
column7: FBI agent delivers defendant's character to the jury.

column5: I said something to him. Do you recall what I said?
column6: Something about understanding they were doing their job / I don't recall exactly.
column7: If recalls: devastating. If doesn't: the handshake itself is enough.

column5: In your training at the FBI Academy, you're taught to assess the demeanor and behavior of subjects and persons of interest?
column7: Establishes professional obligation to observe.

column5: You assessed my demeanor that day.
column7: He saw it. He remembers. The jury now knows what kind of person Alan is. Not from Alan's mouth. From the FBI's.
